tldr: Headlines across 9 country buckets converge on the 1,000-death milestone as the defining anchor for the DRC/Uganda Ebola outbreak; BBC Africa pivots to misinformation driving attacks on health workers, Al Jazeera foregrounds unpaid worker hardship, and RT Africa uniquely reframes the crisis as a geopolitical obstacle to the US minerals deal.

evidence.quote: le bilan dépasse désormais les 1 000 morts
Ebola death toll in DRC and Uganda has surpassed 1,000
Ebola outbreak tops 1,000 deaths as containment
Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 1,000
More than 1,000 people killed in Ebola outbreak
The Race to Contain the Fastest-Ever Ebola Outbreak
'There's no salary': DRC health workers tell of hardship
Ebola misinformation fuels violent attacks on health workers
Africa CDC urges U.S. to lift Uganda Ebola travel
Canada bars foreign travelers over Ebola outbreak
US minerals push hits Ebola roadblock in DR Congo

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet to reframe the Ebola crisis as a geopolitical obstacle — foregrounding disruption to the US-backed critical minerals deal rather than the humanitarian death toll or public-health response.

Only headline to foreground a named physician's voice and open-ended temporal uncertainty ('No One Knows When It Will End') rather than a statistical milestone, humanizing the emergency in a register absent from every other outlet.

bottom_line: Headline coverage locks onto the 1,000-death milestone as the universal anchor, but fractures between humanizing the response failure (worker hardship, misinformation attacks) and geopolitical or travel-policy instrumentalization of the crisis.
